## Why
Short-form/chapter evaluations were being forced into `review_gate / awaiting_approval` after Phase 1A, which is product-inappropriate for chapter-scale submissions (e.g. 4,412 words).

Policy target:
- Internal Story Layer artifact generation can remain enabled.
- User-facing Story Ledger approval gate must be long-form only.

## What changed

### 1) Processor routing policy (hard gate)
In `lib/evaluation/processor.ts`:
- Added `STORY_LEDGER_USER_GATE_MIN_WORDS = 25_000`
- Added `shouldRequireStoryLedgerReviewGate(manuscriptWords)` helper.
- In Phase 1A handoff:
  - If `manuscript_words < 25_000` and handoff readiness is valid:
    - Skip user-facing Review Gate.
    - Auto-write `accepted_story_ledger_v1` with governance rail marking short-form auto-approval.
    - Transition directly to `phase_2 / queued`.
  - If `manuscript_words >= 25_000`, keep existing Review Gate behavior.

### 2) UX wording correction at 15–34%
In `components/evaluation-poller-display.ts`:
- Early phase_1a label changed from **"Reading your manuscript..."** to **"Diagnosing your manuscript..."**
- Helper text now reflects diagnosis/analysis language.

## Tests
- Added threshold regression test in `__tests__/lib/evaluation/processor.test.ts`:
  - `< 25,000` => no user-facing Story Ledger gate
  - `>= 25,000` => user-facing Story Ledger gate required
- Updated `tests/evaluation-poller-display.test.ts` label expectations.

Run locally:
- `npm test -- __tests__/lib/evaluation/processor.test.ts tests/evaluation-poller-display.test.ts --runInBand`
- Result: **59 passed, 0 failed**

## Expected product behavior after merge
- 4.4k chapter evaluations no longer stop at `review_gate / awaiting_approval`.
- They proceed directly into criteria diagnosis pipeline after internal checks.
- Long-form evaluations (>=25k) still use user-facing Story Ledger gate.
